context: Tests whether the agent sends a 200 response immediately (before heavy processing), uses X-Shopify-Webhook-Id as an idempotency key in both the database store and as the Bull jobId, configures retries with exponential backoff, and uses a queue to offload processing.

checklist.name: Immediate 200 response
checklist.description: The route handler calls `res.status(200).json(...)` or equivalent BEFORE awaiting any downstream processing (ERP sync, inventory update, queue operations that do actual work)

checklist.name: X-Shopify-Webhook-Id header used
checklist.description: The handler reads the `x-shopify-webhook-id` request header to obtain the webhook ID

checklist.name: Idempotency check before processing
checklist.description: The handler checks whether the webhook ID has already been processed (via the processed-webhooks store or equivalent) and skips processing if it has

checklist.name: webhookId as Bull jobId
checklist.description: The Bull `queue.add(...)` call includes `jobId: webhookId` (or equivalent) to prevent duplicate job entries for the same webhook

checklist.name: Bull queue used for async processing
checklist.description: Processing (ERP sync, inventory, notifications) happens inside a Bull `queue.process(...)` worker, not inline in the HTTP handler

checklist.name: Retry attempts configured
checklist.description: The Bull job is added with `attempts` set to a value greater than 1

checklist.name: Exponential backoff configured
checklist.description: The Bull job specifies `backoff: { type: 'exponential', ... }` for retry delays

checklist.name: shop domain captured
checklist.description: The handler reads the `x-shopify-shop-domain` header to identify the shop

checklist.name: Processed record stored
checklist.description: After deciding to process a webhook, the handler records the webhookId and shop in the processed-webhooks store before or during processing (not just checking)

checklist.name: README addresses timing
checklist.description: The README explains that the 200 response is sent before processing to meet Shopify's response timeout requirement

checklist.name: README addresses duplicates
checklist.description: The README explains that duplicate deliveries are handled via the idempotency key (webhookId)
